title: Refactor /api/classify SSE to parallel classification
description: Fan out per-track classification through the shared ThreadPool. Cold tracks (no cache hit) compute in parallel; warm tracks (cache hit) return immediately. Cache integration depends on TASK-014.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/classify (and ?playlist_id=N) streams the existing event shape.
Cold-path throughput >= 30 tracks/sec; warm-path throughput >= 500 tracks/sec.
Per-track exceptions emit an error event for that content_id and the scan continues.
Existing classify tests in tests/test_classify.py and tests/test_serve_routes.py still pass.
Memory growth during a 10k-track classify run stays bounded (no future-list bloat).

steps.description: Identify the per-track classification call site
steps.details: In serve/routes.py find the /api/classify SSE handler. It iterates DjmdContent rows and calls classify.get_classification(content, db) for each. Factor that body into _classify_one(content, db).
Submit _classify_one through the pool with a 2*pool_size() in-flight cap. Yield events in completion order using concurrent.futures.as_completed().

Leave the call to classify.get_classification() as-is; once TASK-014 lands, that function reads CacheStore first, so warm tracks return in microseconds and the pool naturally absorbs only the cold work.

Update tests/test_serve_routes.py classify cases to assert set-equality on content_ids rather than order. Add a single test mocking 100 tracks where 80 return from cache (instant) and 20 require compute - verify the wall-clock is dominated by the 20 cold tracks, not 100x cold.
RUN_PERF=1 pytest tests/perf/test_classify.py. Run once with empty cache (cold), once with warm cache (after TASK-014). Confirm both budgets.

technicalNotes: The classify module's _class_cache is per-process; it stays warm within a single autocue serve run. Sidecar cache (TASK-014) gives cross-run warmth.
